LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARIA IRMA LOPEZ DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. --- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS OVERRULED. THE DECLARATORY RELIEF SOUGHT BY PLAINTIFF UNDER SECTION 1060 (TO DECLARE THE LEASES VOID) IS NOT WITHIN THE JURISDICTION OF THE SF RENT BOARD. ACCORDINGLY, PLAINTIFF IS NOT REQUIRED TO EXHAUST ANY ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES AT THE RENT BOARD BEFORE BRINGING AN ACTION FOR THAT DECLARATORY RELIEF IN THIS COURT. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: CARLITO MANDIA, CSR 	689

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS GABRIELLA OCAMPO AND PEDRO PADILLA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEMURRER OVERRULED. 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CLAIM SEEKING A DECLARATION THAT ENFORCEMENT OF THE LEASES AGAINST PLAINTIFF WOULD CONSTITUTE AN UNCONSTITUTIONAL TAKING. THERE ARE NO ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES THAT PLAINTIFF WAS ENTITLED TO EXHAUST. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. IN ADDITION, COURT SIGNED PLAINTIFF'S ORDER OVERRULING DEFENDANT MARIA IRMA LOPEZ' DEMURRER RE 06/02/11 HEARING. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Oct-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Paul Utrecht, Esq., from Zacks and Utrecht, 415-956-5100, for Plaintiff and moving party
William E. Shapiro, Esq., Law Office, 415-678-9209, for Defendant and opposing party: Maria I. Lopez

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
PLAINTIFF FOUNDERS REALTY, INC. AND RAYMOND GRINSELL, TRUSTEE OF EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MANAGEMENT'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is denied without prejudice to refiling the motion with proper supporting papers. Plaintiff failed to file and serve a separate statement with its motion for summary adjudication. The lack of a separate statement is prejudicial and violates the due process purpose of the statement because the federal defense is factual in nature. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Defendants Pedro Padilla, Gabriella Ocampo Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-17-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS GABRIELLA OCAMPO AND PEDRO PADILLA'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: James Brandan Kraus, Esq. 956-8100 for pltf & opposing party; Russell Davis, Esq. of Law Offices of Russell Davis 409-5627 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - DEMURRER OVERRULED. 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CLAIM SEEKING A DECLARATION THAT ENFORCEMENT OF THE LEASES AGAINST PLAINTIFF WOULD CONSTITUTE AN UNCONSTITUTIONAL TAKING. THERE ARE NO ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES THAT PLAINTIFF WAS ENTITLED TO EXHAUST. Order signed in open court. In addition, Court signed plaintiff's order overruling defendant Maria Irma Lopez' demurrer re 06/02/11 hearing. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS PEDRO PADILLA, GABRIELLA OCAMPO DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

RULING: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling - CONTINUED TO JUNE 17, 2011 TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE GIORGI. (302/EHG) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Defendant Maria Lopez' Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-02-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT MARIA IRMA LOPEZ DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Andrew M. Zacks, Esq. of Zacks & Utrecht 956-8100 for pltfs & opposing parties; William E. Shapiro, Esq. of Law Offices of William E. Shapiro 678-9209 & Mary Catherine Wiederhold, Esq. of Law Offices of Mary Catherine Wiederhold 533-0735 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Demurrer is overruled. The declaratory relief sought by plaintiff under section 1060 (to declare the leases void) is not within the jurisdiction of the SF Rent Board. Accordingly, plaintiff is not required to exhaust any administrative remedies at the Rent Board before bringing an action for that declaratory relief in this Court. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Carlito Mandia, CSR 689
